title: Model
description: This schema defines the Model part of a service description, part of a service description that tells a service requester how to construct an invocation message and interpret a response message.

interface.description: A named set (logical grouping) of operations.

category.description: The type of the interface the service deploys.

operation.description: Zero or more operations offered by the interface.

synchronicity.description: A value that indicates whether the operation is synchronous or not (asynchronous).

precondition.description: A description of the state or condition that should be true before the operation can proceed.

input.description: The name of the input message that initiates interaction with the operation.

output.description: The name of the output message produced in response to a service request.

effect.description: A description of the state or condition that exists after the operation is completed, assuming no error has occurred.

message-exchange-pattern.description: A value that indicates the pattern of message exchange between interacting components.

message.description: Zero or more messages associated with the operation.

direction.description: A value that indicates whether the message is input (in) or output (out).

header.description: A collection of zero or more name-value pairs (fields) that provides information about how the message should be processed or interpreted.

name.description: The name of the header field.

description.description: A description of the header field.

permissible-value.description: A value of the header field.

payload.description: The data entity transferred by a message.

payload-type.description: A value that indicates the format of the data transferred by the message.

name.description: The name given to the data entity that constitutes the payload.

description.description: A description of the payload data entity.

data-specification.description: One or more document that defines and describes the meaning, structure, inter-relationships, permissible values, and other aspects of a data entity.

formal-language.description: The language in which the document is written.

information-exchange-model.description: Name of the information exchange model that the payload data conforms to.

source.description: A document from which the data specification is derived or cited.

resource.description: An information item identified by a URI that a client can interact with through a set of standard methods.

id.description: The unique persistent resource identifier presented as URI.

path.description: A relative path to the URI that identifies the resource in the context of the API.

template.description: A resource URI syntax that includes variables (parameters) that must be substituted before the URI is resolved.

parameter-type.description: A value that indicates the type of the parameter.

permissible-value.description: A set of values (zero or more) that the parameter may accept.
